messages.content: What are all the available sub-agents in the AssetOpsBench system?
The available sub-agents are: IoTAgent, FMSRAgent, TSFMAgent, Utilities, WorkOrderAgent, VibrationAgent.
What tools does IoTAgent provide?
IoTAgent provides these tools: sites, assets, sensors, history. Handles IoT telemetry data access — sites, assets, sensors, and historical readings from industrial equipment.
What tools does FMSRAgent provide?
FMSRAgent provides these tools: get_failure_modes, get_failure_mode_sensor_mapping. Provides failure mode analysis and sensor relevance reasoning for industrial assets.
What tools does TSFMAgent provide?

TSFMAgent provides these tools: get_ai_tasks, get_tsfm_models, run_tsfm_forecasting, run_tsfm_finetuning, run_tsad, run_integrated_tsad. Time-series forecasting and anomaly detection using IBM Granite TinyTimeMixer models.
What tools does Utilities provide?
Utilities provides these tools: json_reader, current_date_time, current_time_english. General utility functions — file reading, date/time queries.
What tools does WorkOrderAgent provide?

WorkOrderAgent provides these tools: get_work_orders, get_preventive_work_orders, get_corrective_work_orders, get_events, get_failure_codes, get_work_order_distribution, predict_next_work_order, analyze_alert_to_failure. Work order and maintenance event reasoning — queries historical maintenance records, failure codes, and predicts future work orders.
What tools does VibrationAgent provide?

VibrationAgent provides these tools: get_vibration_data, list_vibration_sensors, compute_fft_spectrum, compute_envelope_spectrum, assess_vibration_severity, calculate_bearing_frequencies, list_known_bearings, diagnose_vibration. Vibration analysis for rotating equipment — FFT spectra, envelope analysis, bearing diagnostics, severity assessment.
Which agent owns the 'sites' tool?
The 'sites' tool belongs to IoTAgent. It list all available iot sites.
Which agent owns the 'assets' tool?
The 'assets' tool belongs to IoTAgent. It list all asset ids for a given site.
Which agent owns the 'sensors' tool?
The 'sensors' tool belongs to IoTAgent. It list sensor names for a specific asset at a site.
Which agent owns the 'history' tool?
The 'history' tool belongs to IoTAgent. It fetch historical sensor readings for a time range.
Which agent owns the 'get_failure_modes' tool?
The 'get_failure_modes' tool belongs to FMSRAgent. It return known failure modes for an asset type.
Which agent owns the 'get_failure_mode_sensor_mapping' tool?
The 'get_failure_mode_sensor_mapping' tool belongs to FMSRAgent. It for each failure mode and sensor pair, determine relevancy. returns bidirectional mappings..
Which agent owns the 'get_ai_tasks' tool?
The 'get_ai_tasks' tool belongs to TSFMAgent. It list supported ai task types for time-series analysis.
Which agent owns the 'get_tsfm_models' tool?
The 'get_tsfm_models' tool belongs to TSFMAgent. It list available pre-trained tinytimemixer model checkpoints.
Which agent owns the 'run_tsfm_forecasting' tool?
The 'run_tsfm_forecasting' tool belongs to TSFMAgent. It run zero-shot time-series forecasting with a ttm model.
Which agent owns the 'run_tsfm_finetuning' tool?
The 'run_tsfm_finetuning' tool belongs to TSFMAgent. It few-shot fine-tune a ttm model on domain data.
Which agent owns the 'run_tsad' tool?
The 'run_tsad' tool belongs to TSFMAgent. It conformal anomaly detection on top of forecasting output.
Which agent owns the 'run_integrated_tsad' tool?
The 'run_integrated_tsad' tool belongs to TSFMAgent. It end-to-end forecasting + anomaly detection in one call.
Which agent owns the 'json_reader' tool?
The 'json_reader' tool belongs to Utilities. It read and parse a json file from disk.
Which agent owns the 'current_date_time' tool?
The 'current_date_time' tool belongs to Utilities. It return the current utc date and time as json.
Which agent owns the 'current_time_english' tool?
The 'current_time_english' tool belongs to Utilities. It return the current utc time as a human-readable string.
Which agent owns the 'get_work_orders' tool?
The 'get_work_orders' tool belongs to WorkOrderAgent. It retrieve work orders for an asset, optionally filtered by date range.
Which agent owns the 'get_preventive_work_orders' tool?
The 'get_preventive_work_orders' tool belongs to WorkOrderAgent. It retrieve preventive maintenance work orders for an asset.
Which agent owns the 'get_corrective_work_orders' tool?
The 'get_corrective_work_orders' tool belongs to WorkOrderAgent. It retrieve corrective/reactive work orders for an asset.
Which agent owns the 'get_events' tool?
The 'get_events' tool belongs to WorkOrderAgent. It retrieve maintenance events and alerts for an asset.
Which agent owns the 'get_failure_codes' tool?
The 'get_failure_codes' tool belongs to WorkOrderAgent. It retrieve failure codes associated with an asset's work orders.
Which agent owns the 'get_work_order_distribution' tool?
The 'get_work_order_distribution' tool belongs to WorkOrderAgent. It get distribution/statistics of work orders by type, priority, or time period.
Which agent owns the 'predict_next_work_order' tool?
The 'predict_next_work_order' tool belongs to WorkOrderAgent. It predict when the next work order will be needed for an asset.
Which agent owns the 'analyze_alert_to_failure' tool?
The 'analyze_alert_to_failure' tool belongs to WorkOrderAgent. It analyze the relationship between alerts/events and subsequent failures.
Which agent owns the 'get_vibration_data' tool?
The 'get_vibration_data' tool belongs to VibrationAgent. It retrieve raw vibration measurement data for a sensor.
Which agent owns the 'list_vibration_sensors' tool?
The 'list_vibration_sensors' tool belongs to VibrationAgent. It list all vibration sensors for a given asset.
Which agent owns the 'compute_fft_spectrum' tool?
The 'compute_fft_spectrum' tool belongs to VibrationAgent. It compute fft frequency spectrum from vibration data.
Which agent owns the 'compute_envelope_spectrum' tool?
The 'compute_envelope_spectrum' tool belongs to VibrationAgent. It compute envelope spectrum for bearing fault detection.
Which agent owns the 'assess_vibration_severity' tool?
The 'assess_vibration_severity' tool belongs to VibrationAgent. It assess vibration severity against iso standards.
Which agent owns the 'calculate_bearing_frequencies' tool?
The 'calculate_bearing_frequencies' tool belongs to VibrationAgent. It calculate characteristic bearing fault frequencies.
Which agent owns the 'list_known_bearings' tool?
The 'list_known_bearings' tool belongs to VibrationAgent. It list known bearing models in the database.
Which agent owns the 'diagnose_vibration' tool?
The 'diagnose_vibration' tool belongs to VibrationAgent. It run comprehensive vibration diagnosis for an asset.
What arguments does the IoTAgent.sites tool accept?
The sites tool (IoTAgent) takes no arguments. It returns: List of site names.
What arguments does the IoTAgent.assets tool accept?
The assets tool (IoTAgent) accepts: site_name: str - Name of the site. It returns: List of asset names/IDs at the site.
What arguments does the IoTAgent.sensors tool accept?
The sensors tool (IoTAgent) accepts: site_name: str - Name of the site; asset_id: str - Asset identifier. It returns: List of sensor names.
What arguments does the IoTAgent.history tool accept?

The history tool (IoTAgent) accepts: site_name: str - Name of the site; asset_id: str - Asset identifier; start: str - ISO 8601 start timestamp; final: str (optional) - ISO 8601 end timestamp. It returns: Time-series sensor data as JSON.
What arguments does the FMSRAgent.get_failure_modes tool accept?
The get_failure_modes tool (FMSRAgent) accepts: asset_name: str - Type of asset (e.g. 'chiller', 'ahu'). It returns: List of failure modes with descriptions.
What arguments does the FMSRAgent.get_failure_mode_sensor_mapping tool accept?

The get_failure_mode_sensor_mapping tool (FMSRAgent) accepts: asset_name: str - Type of asset; failure_modes: list[str] - Failure modes to check; sensors: list[str] - Sensors to check against. It returns: Bidirectional fm->sensors and sensor->fms maps with relevancy details.
What arguments does the TSFMAgent.get_ai_tasks tool accept?
The get_ai_tasks tool (TSFMAgent) takes no arguments. It returns: List of task types (forecasting, anomaly detection, etc.).
What arguments does the TSFMAgent.get_tsfm_models tool accept?
The get_tsfm_models tool (TSFMAgent) takes no arguments. It returns: List of model checkpoint names.
What arguments does the TSFMAgent.run_tsfm_forecasting tool accept?

The run_tsfm_forecasting tool (TSFMAgent) accepts: dataset_path: str - Path to the dataset CSV; timestamp_column: str - Name of the timestamp column; target_columns: list[str] - Columns to forecast; model_checkpoint: str (optional) - Model to use; forecast_horizon: int (optional) - Number of steps to forecast. It returns: Path to JSON predictions file.
What arguments does the TSFMAgent.run_tsfm_finetuning tool accept?

The run_tsfm_finetuning tool (TSFMAgent) accepts: dataset_path: str - Path to the dataset CSV; timestamp_column: str - Name of the timestamp column; target_columns: list[str] - Columns to fine-tune on; model_checkpoint: str (optional) - Base model to fine-tune; save_model_dir: str (optional) - Where to save the fine-tuned model. It returns: Saved checkpoint path and metrics.
What arguments does the TSFMAgent.run_tsad tool accept?

The run_tsad tool (TSFMAgent) accepts: dataset_path: str - Path to the dataset CSV; tsfm_output_json: str - Path to forecasting output JSON; timestamp_column: str - Name of the timestamp column; target_columns: list[str] - Columns to check for anomalies. It returns: CSV with anomaly labels.
What arguments does the TSFMAgent.run_integrated_tsad tool accept?

The run_integrated_tsad tool (TSFMAgent) accepts: dataset_path: str - Path to the dataset CSV; timestamp_column: str - Name of the timestamp column; target_columns: list[str] - Columns to analyze; model_checkpoint: str (optional) - Model to use; false_alarm: float (optional) - False alarm rate threshold. It returns: Combined CSV with forecasts and anomaly labels.
What arguments does the Utilities.json_reader tool accept?
The json_reader tool (Utilities) accepts: file_name: str - Path to JSON file. It returns: Parsed JSON content.
What arguments does the Utilities.current_date_time tool accept?
The current_date_time tool (Utilities) takes no arguments. It returns: JSON with currentDateTime and description.
What arguments does the Utilities.current_time_english tool accept?
The current_time_english tool (Utilities) takes no arguments. It returns: Human-readable date/time string.
What arguments does the WorkOrderAgent.get_work_orders tool accept?
The get_work_orders tool (WorkOrderAgent) accepts: asset_name: str - Asset identifier; start_date: str (optional); end_date: str (optional). It returns: List of work order records.
What arguments does the WorkOrderAgent.get_preventive_work_orders tool accept?
The get_preventive_work_orders tool (WorkOrderAgent) accepts: asset_name: str - Asset identifier. It returns: List of preventive work orders.
What arguments does the WorkOrderAgent.get_corrective_work_orders tool accept?
The get_corrective_work_orders tool (WorkOrderAgent) accepts: asset_name: str - Asset identifier. It returns: List of corrective work orders.
What arguments does the WorkOrderAgent.get_events tool accept?
The get_events tool (WorkOrderAgent) accepts: asset_name: str - Asset identifier. It returns: List of events/alerts.
What arguments does the WorkOrderAgent.get_failure_codes tool accept?
The get_failure_codes tool (WorkOrderAgent) accepts: asset_name: str - Asset identifier. It returns: List of failure codes with descriptions.
What arguments does the WorkOrderAgent.get_work_order_distribution tool accept?
The get_work_order_distribution tool (WorkOrderAgent) accepts: asset_name: str - Asset identifier; group_by: str (optional) - Grouping dimension. It returns: Work order distribution statistics.
What arguments does the WorkOrderAgent.predict_next_work_order tool accept?
The predict_next_work_order tool (WorkOrderAgent) accepts: asset_name: str - Asset identifier. It returns: Predicted date and type of next work order.
What arguments does the WorkOrderAgent.analyze_alert_to_failure tool accept?
The analyze_alert_to_failure tool (WorkOrderAgent) accepts: asset_name: str - Asset identifier. It returns: Alert-to-failure correlation analysis.
What arguments does the VibrationAgent.get_vibration_data tool accept?
The get_vibration_data tool (VibrationAgent) accepts: sensor_id: str - Vibration sensor identifier; start: str (optional); end: str (optional). It returns: Time-series vibration data.
What arguments does the VibrationAgent.list_vibration_sensors tool accept?
The list_vibration_sensors tool (VibrationAgent) accepts: asset_name: str - Asset identifier. It returns: List of vibration sensor IDs and locations.
What arguments does the VibrationAgent.compute_fft_spectrum tool accept?
The compute_fft_spectrum tool (VibrationAgent) accepts: sensor_id: str - Sensor identifier; window_size: int (optional). It returns: Frequency spectrum data.
What arguments does the VibrationAgent.compute_envelope_spectrum tool accept?
The compute_envelope_spectrum tool (VibrationAgent) accepts: sensor_id: str - Sensor identifier. It returns: Envelope spectrum data.
What arguments does the VibrationAgent.assess_vibration_severity tool accept?
The assess_vibration_severity tool (VibrationAgent) accepts: sensor_id: str - Sensor identifier. It returns: Severity classification and RMS values.
What arguments does the VibrationAgent.calculate_bearing_frequencies tool accept?
The calculate_bearing_frequencies tool (VibrationAgent) accepts: bearing_model: str - Bearing model/type; shaft_rpm: float - Shaft rotation speed. It returns: BPFO, BPFI, BSF, FTF frequencies.
What arguments does the VibrationAgent.list_known_bearings tool accept?
The list_known_bearings tool (VibrationAgent) takes no arguments. It returns: List of bearing model numbers and specifications.
What arguments does the VibrationAgent.diagnose_vibration tool accept?
The diagnose_vibration tool (VibrationAgent) accepts: asset_name: str - Asset identifier. It returns: Diagnosis report with fault indicators and recommendations.
Which agent and tool should handle this request: "I need to find out what sites have IoT sensors deployed."
Agent: IoTAgent, Tool: sites. List all available IoT sites.
Which agent and tool should handle this request: "List the equipment at the MAIN facility."
Agent: IoTAgent, Tool: assets. List all asset IDs for a given site.
Which agent and tool should handle this request: "What sensors are installed on Chiller 6?"
Agent: IoTAgent, Tool: sensors. List sensor names for a specific asset at a site.
Which agent and tool should handle this request: "Get temperature readings for Chiller 6 from January 2020."
Agent: IoTAgent, Tool: history. Fetch historical sensor readings for a time range.
Which agent and tool should handle this request: "What can go wrong with a chiller?"
Agent: FMSRAgent, Tool: get_failure_modes. Return known failure modes for an asset type.
Which agent and tool should handle this request: "Which sensors can detect compressor overheating?"
Agent: FMSRAgent, Tool: get_failure_mode_sensor_mapping. For each failure mode and sensor pair, determine relevancy. Returns bidirectional mappings..
Which agent and tool should handle this request: "What time-series AI tasks are available?"
Agent: TSFMAgent, Tool: get_ai_tasks. List supported AI task types for time-series analysis.
Which agent and tool should handle this request: "Which forecasting models can I use?"
Agent: TSFMAgent, Tool: get_tsfm_models. List available pre-trained TinyTimeMixer model checkpoints.
Which agent and tool should handle this request: "Run a forecast on the chiller sensor data."
Agent: TSFMAgent, Tool: run_tsfm_forecasting. Run zero-shot time-series forecasting with a TTM model.
Which agent and tool should handle this request: "Fine-tune the time-series model on my dataset."
Agent: TSFMAgent, Tool: run_tsfm_finetuning. Few-shot fine-tune a TTM model on domain data.
Which agent and tool should handle this request: "Detect anomalies in the sensor readings."
Agent: TSFMAgent, Tool: run_integrated_tsad. End-to-end forecasting + anomaly detection in one call.
Which agent and tool should handle this request: "What is the current date and time?"
Agent: Utilities, Tool: current_date_time. Return the current UTC date and time as JSON.
Which agent and tool should handle this request: "Read the configuration file."
Agent: Utilities, Tool: json_reader. Read and parse a JSON file from disk.
Which agent and tool should handle this request: "Show me work orders for Chiller 6."
Agent: WorkOrderAgent, Tool: get_work_orders. Retrieve work orders for an asset, optionally filtered by date range.
Which agent and tool should handle this request: "What preventive maintenance is scheduled?"
Agent: WorkOrderAgent, Tool: get_preventive_work_orders. Retrieve preventive maintenance work orders for an asset.
Which agent and tool should handle this request: "When is the next work order predicted?"
Agent: WorkOrderAgent, Tool: predict_next_work_order. Predict when the next work order will be needed for an asset.
Which agent and tool should handle this request: "What failure codes appear most often?"
Agent: WorkOrderAgent, Tool: get_failure_codes. Retrieve failure codes associated with an asset's work orders.
Which agent and tool should handle this request: "Analyze the correlation between alerts and failures."
Agent: WorkOrderAgent, Tool: analyze_alert_to_failure. Analyze the relationship between alerts/events and subsequent failures.
Which agent and tool should handle this request: "Get vibration data for the compressor."
Agent: VibrationAgent, Tool: get_vibration_data. Retrieve raw vibration measurement data for a sensor.
Which agent and tool should handle this request: "Compute the FFT spectrum for bearing analysis."
Agent: VibrationAgent, Tool: compute_fft_spectrum. Compute FFT frequency spectrum from vibration data.
Which agent and tool should handle this request: "Run a vibration diagnosis on the pump."
Agent: VibrationAgent, Tool: diagnose_vibration. Run comprehensive vibration diagnosis for an asset.
Which agent and tool should handle this request: "What bearing models are in the database?"
Agent: VibrationAgent, Tool: list_known_bearings. List known bearing models in the database.
Which agent and tool should handle this request: "Assess vibration severity for sensor V-101."
Agent: VibrationAgent, Tool: assess_vibration_severity. Assess vibration severity against ISO standards.
A system incorrectly routed the query "I need sensor data for Chiller 6." to FMSRAgent.get_failure_modes. What is the correct routing and why?
Incorrect: FMSRAgent.get_failure_modes. Correct: IoTAgent.sensors. This is a sensor data query, not a failure mode query. Use IoTAgent.sensors to list sensors, or IoTAgent.history for readings.
A system incorrectly routed the query "Predict when the chiller will need maintenance." to IoTAgent.history. What is the correct routing and why?
Incorrect: IoTAgent.history. Correct: WorkOrderAgent.predict_next_work_order. Maintenance prediction requires work order analysis, not raw sensor history. Use WorkOrderAgent.predict_next_work_order.
A system incorrectly routed the query "What failure modes affect the AHU?" to IoTAgent.assets. What is the correct routing and why?
Incorrect: IoTAgent.assets. Correct: FMSRAgent.get_failure_modes. Failure mode information comes from FMSRAgent, not IoTAgent. IoTAgent handles telemetry data, not failure analysis.
A system incorrectly routed the query "Run anomaly detection on the pump." to FMSRAgent.get_failure_mode_sensor_mapping. What is the correct routing and why?

Incorrect: FMSRAgent.get_failure_mode_sensor_mapping. Correct: TSFMAgent.run_integrated_tsad. Anomaly detection is a time-series task handled by TSFMAgent, not FMSRAgent. FMSRAgent handles failure mode analysis.
A system incorrectly routed the query "What is the FFT spectrum for the motor bearing?" to TSFMAgent.run_tsfm_forecasting. What is the correct routing and why?
Incorrect: TSFMAgent.run_tsfm_forecasting. Correct: VibrationAgent.compute_fft_spectrum. FFT spectrum computation is a vibration analysis task. Use VibrationAgent.compute_fft_spectrum, not TSFMAgent.
A system incorrectly routed the query "Get corrective work orders for Chiller 6." to IoTAgent.history. What is the correct routing and why?
Incorrect: IoTAgent.history. Correct: WorkOrderAgent.get_corrective_work_orders. Work order retrieval requires WorkOrderAgent, not IoTAgent. IoTAgent handles sensor data, not maintenance records.
Get sensor data.
This query is incomplete. I need to know: which site (e.g., MAIN), which asset (e.g., Chiller 6), and optionally a time range. Please provide more details.
Run the model.

This query is ambiguous. Do you want to: (a) run forecasting with TSFMAgent.run_tsfm_forecasting, (b) run anomaly detection with TSFMAgent.run_integrated_tsad, or (c) fine-tune a model with TSFMAgent.run_tsfm_finetuning? Please specify the task and dataset.
What happened to the equipment?
This query is too vague. Please specify: (a) which equipment/asset, (b) what type of information you need (sensor data, work orders, failure modes, events), and (c) a time period if relevant.
Fix the chiller.

I cannot execute physical maintenance actions. I can help you with: (a) diagnosing the issue using sensor data (IoTAgent), (b) identifying failure modes (FMSRAgent), (c) predicting when maintenance is needed (WorkOrderAgent), or (d) detecting anomalies (TSFMAgent). What analysis would be helpful?
Is the pump going to fail?

To assess failure risk, I need more context: (a) which specific pump/asset, (b) at which site, and (c) what type of analysis — anomaly detection on sensor data (TSFMAgent), failure mode analysis (FMSRAgent), or work order history (WorkOrderAgent)?
We need to get a comprehensive list of all active IoT monitoring facilities across our global operations. Which tool is best suited for this task?
You should use `IoTAgent.sites` to retrieve a list of all available IoT monitoring sites.
What input parameters or arguments does the `IoTAgent.sites` function require to execute successfully?
The `IoTAgent.sites` tool does not require any arguments; it will list all available IoT monitoring sites directly.
I need to quickly see an overview of all our currently connected industrial sites that are sending telemetry data. Can you help me with that?
Absolutely. You can use the `IoTAgent.sites` tool to list all available IoT monitoring sites.
We need to get a complete list of all active asset IDs for the new solar farm in Arizona. Which tool is best suited for this task?
You should use the `IoTAgent.assets` tool to retrieve the asset IDs for a specific industrial site.
When using the `IoTAgent.assets` tool, how do I specify which particular facility or plant I'm interested in?
You need to provide the `site_name` as a string argument to the tool, like `IoTAgent.assets(site_name='Arizona_Solar_Farm')`.
I need to generate an inventory report detailing every piece of equipment at our offshore oil rig, identified by its asset ID. Can you help me get that data?
Yes, you can use the `IoTAgent.assets` tool, providing the name of your offshore oil rig as the `site_name` argument to list all its asset IDs.
A maintenance engineer needs to identify all the active telemetry points on a specific pump at the 'Midwest Pumping Station'. Which tool would be most appropriate for this task?
You should use the `IoTAgent.sensors` tool. It's designed to list all sensor names associated with a given industrial asset at a specific site.
When utilizing the `IoTAgent.sensors` tool, what parameters are required to successfully retrieve a list of sensors?
To get the sensor list, you must provide the `site_name` where the asset is located and the unique `asset_id` of the equipment in question.
I need to get a comprehensive list of all sensors connected to the 'Compressor-A7' asset at our 'North Plant' facility. Can you help me fetch that information?
Absolutely. You can obtain this by invoking `IoTAgent.sensors` with `site_name='North Plant'` and `asset_id='Compressor-A7'`.
I'm trying to understand the typical malfunctions and breakdowns associated with a specific type of industrial pump in our facility. Which tool should I use for this analysis?
You should use `FMSRAgent.get_failure_modes` to retrieve a list of known failure modes for that particular asset type.
What input parameter is required when calling the `FMSRAgent.get_failure_modes` function?
The `get_failure_modes` function requires a single string argument, `asset_name`, which specifies the exact industrial asset type you are querying.
Help me identify common operational issues for a gas turbine.
To identify common operational issues, invoke `FMSRAgent.get_failure_modes` with `asset_name='gas turbine'`.

Our predictive maintenance system needs to correlate specific failure modes like bearing wear or cavitation with the most relevant operational sensors on our centrifugal pump. Which tool should I use for this analysis?
You should use
Our predictive maintenance system needs to identify the available TinyTimeMixer model checkpoints for anomaly detection on pump vibration data. Which tool should I use to get that list?
You should use `TSFMAgent.get_tsfm_models` to list all available time-series forecasting and anomaly detection model checkpoints.
What arguments are necessary when I call `TSFMAgent.get_tsfm_models` to retrieve the list of available models?
The `TSFMAgent.get_tsfm_models` tool does not require any arguments to list the available model checkpoints.
I need to quickly see which time-series forecasting models are currently ready for deployment on our industrial sensor data. Can you help me with that?
Absolutely. You can use `TSFMAgent.get_tsfm_models` to retrieve a comprehensive list of all available time-series forecasting and anomaly detection model checkpoints.

Our predictive maintenance system has generated some forecasts for our turbine's vibration levels, and I need to identify if any of these predicted values represent a significant anomaly. Which tool should I use for this?
To perform conformal anomaly detection on your turbine's forecasted vibration levels, you should use `TSFMAgent.run_tsad`.
I'm trying to run the `TSFMAgent.run_tsad` tool, but I'm unsure about the `tsfm_output_json` parameter. What kind of file path does it require?
The `tsfm_output_json` argument requires the file path to the JSON output from your time-series forecasting model, which includes the predicted values for anomaly detection.
Help me! I need to quickly detect any abnormal patterns in the forecasted temperature of our chemical reactor, based on the TinyTimeMixer's predictions.
You can achieve this by invoking `TSFMA

Our critical pump's flow rate sensor data is showing some unusual fluctuations. What's the most suitable tool to not only predict future flow rates but also pinpoint any anomalous readings that could indicate an impending failure?
For integrated forecasting and anomaly detection on your pump's flow rate time-series data, you should use `TSFMAgent.run_integrated_tsad`.
I'm trying to configure `TSFMAgent.run_integrated_tsad` for our manufacturing line's throughput data. What specific arguments does this tool require to process the dataset?

The `TSFMAgent.run_integrated_tsad` tool requires the `dataset_path` to your throughput data, the name of the `timestamp_column`, and a list of `target_columns` representing the specific throughput metrics you want to analyze.

I need to establish a robust monitoring system for our industrial robots, capable of detecting any abnormal joint torque values and providing projections for future torque behavior to optimize our maintenance schedule.

You can accomplish this by calling `TSFMAgent.run_integrated_tsad`, providing the file path to your robot's joint torque data, specifying the timestamp column, and listing the relevant joint torque columns as targets
Our SCADA system just generated a configuration update in a JSON file for a new batch process. Which tool should I use to ingest and parse this file into the system?
You should use `Utilities.json_reader` to efficiently parse the JSON configuration file.
When utilizing the `Utilities.json_reader` to process a maintenance schedule, what specific argument is required to specify the file's location?
The `Utilities.json_reader` requires a single string argument named `file_name`, which should be the full path to your JSON file.
Help me, I need to load the historical sensor data from `plant_floor_temps.json` for my trend analysis. How do I go about this?
To load that data, simply invoke `Utilities.json_reader` and pass `plant_floor_temps.json` as the `file_name` argument.
We need to accurately timestamp a critical alarm event in our SCADA system for post-incident analysis. Which tool should I use to get the precise UTC time for this entry?
You should use `Utilities.current_date_time` to retrieve the current UTC date and time for your alarm record.
When I'm using `Utilities.current_date_time` to log a maintenance intervention, do I need to pass any specific arguments, like a timezone or a format string?
No, the `Utilities.current_date_time` tool takes no arguments; it simply returns the current UTC date and time as JSON.
I need to quickly get the current UTC timestamp right now to mark the start of this emergency shutdown procedure. Can you help me with that?
Certainly. You can use the `Utilities.current_date_time` tool to retrieve the exact current UTC date and time for your procedure log.
For our shift handover logs, I need to accurately record the current UTC time of a process parameter deviation. Which tool would be best suited for obtaining this timestamp?
You should use
I need to review all pending maintenance tasks for the 'Boiler-005' asset. Which tool should I use to retrieve this information?
You should use the `WorkOrderAgent.get_work_orders` tool to fetch all relevant work orders for 'Boiler-005'.
When utilizing `WorkOrderAgent.get_work_orders` to check historical work orders for a critical pump, are the date parameters always required?
No, only the `asset_name` is a mandatory argument. The `start_date` and `end_date` are optional and can be omitted if you need all work orders for the
I need to identify all upcoming scheduled maintenance tasks for the 'Main Compressor Unit 3'. Which tool should I use to retrieve this information?
Use `WorkOrder
I need to analyze the unscheduled downtime events for our 'HVAC_System_CRAC_01'. Which function should I use to pull all the reactive work orders associated with it?
You should use `WorkOrderAgent.get_corrective_work_orders` to retrieve those specific reactive maintenance records.
Our #3 compressor unit, 'Atlas-C3', has been experiencing repeated trips. What's the best way to pull up its historical failure codes to identify common issues?
You should use the `WorkOrderAgent.get_failure_codes` tool. It's designed specifically for retrieving failure codes from past work orders for a given asset.
When I invoke `WorkOrderAgent.get_failure_codes`, what specific information does it require to execute the query successfully?
The tool requires a single string argument: `asset_name`. You need to provide the precise identifier or name of the industrial asset you wish to query.
I need to quickly review all recorded failure codes for the 'Boiler-HX-001' heat exchanger to prepare for our root cause analysis meeting tomorrow. Can you assist?
Absolutely. You can use the `WorkOrderAgent.get_failure_codes` tool, specifying 'Boiler-HX-001' as the asset name
We need to analyze the distribution of maintenance work orders across our critical production lines to identify bottlenecks. Which tool should I use for this kind of statistical overview?
You should use `WorkOrderAgent.get_work_order_distribution` to retrieve the necessary work order statistics for your production assets.

When invoking `get_work_order_distribution`, what parameters are required to get a breakdown of work orders for a specific compressor, and can I categorize the results by the type of maintenance performed?
You must provide the `asset_name` (e.g., 'Compressor-A1'). Yes, you can categorize the results using the optional `group_by` argument, for instance, `group_by='maintenance_type'`.
I need to understand how work orders are distributed for our 'Reactor-B2' asset, specifically grouped by the reported failure codes over the last quarter.
To achieve this, execute `WorkOrderAgent.get_work_order_distribution` with `asset_name

Our predictive maintenance strategy requires us to anticipate future repair needs. If I want to find out when the next work order is projected for a specific critical asset, which tool should I leverage?
You should use `WorkOrderAgent.predict_next_work_order` to determine the projected timing for the next maintenance event.
When utilizing the `predict_next_work_order` function, what specific parameter is required to identify the asset for which the prediction is being made?
You must provide the `asset_name` as a string argument to specify which industrial asset you are querying.

We're seeing a lot of critical alerts from the pump station, but maintenance isn't always proactive. Is there a way to determine if specific alert types consistently precede particular equipment failures, to better prioritize work orders?
Yes, you can use `WorkOrderAgent.analyze_alert_to_failure` to identify historical correlations between sensor alerts and subsequent asset failures, helping you understand predictive patterns.
What input is necessary when utilizing the `analyze_alert_to_failure` function within the WorkOrderAgent?
The `analyze_alert_to_failure` function requires the `asset_name` parameter, specifying the particular industrial asset for which you want to analyze alert-to-failure correlations.

I need to investigate whether the recurring high-temperature alerts on our #3 compressor are actually reliable indicators of impending motor bearing failures. Can you assist me in performing that analysis?

Absolutely. I can invoke `WorkOrderAgent.analyze_alert_to_failure` for the #3 compressor to analyze historical data and determine if there's a correlation between high-temperature alerts and motor bearing failures.
I need to identify all the vibration monitoring instrumentation currently deployed on the 'Turbine Generator Unit 7' asset. Which tool should I use for this task?
You should use `VibrationAgent.list_vibration_sensors` to retrieve a comprehensive list of all vibration sensors associated with that asset.
What specific argument is required when calling `VibrationAgent.list_vibration_sensors` to ensure I get the sensor data for the correct piece of equipment?
You must provide the `asset_name` argument, which specifies the unique identifier of the industrial asset for which you want to list the vibration sensors.
I need to quickly pull a list of all installed vibration sensors on the 'Main Feed Pump P-101' asset. Can you help me with that?
Certainly. To achieve this, simply invoke `VibrationAgent.list_vibration_sensors` and pass 'Main Feed Pump P-101' as the `asset_name`.

We're seeing elevated vibration levels on our main chiller unit and need to convert the raw time-series data into the frequency domain to identify potential fault frequencies. Which tool in the VibrationAgent would be appropriate for this analysis?
You should use `VibrationAgent.compute_fft_spectrum` to generate the frequency spectrum from your vibration data, which is essential for diagnosing machine health issues.
I'm trying to call the `compute_fft_spectrum` function. What specific argument does it require to process the vibration data from a particular measurement point?
The `compute_fft_spectrum` function requires a `sensor_id` (string) to specify which sensor's vibration data should be used to compute the FFT spectrum.
Hey, I need to quickly get an FFT spectrum for the vibration sensor on the gearbox output shaft of pump P-101. Can you help me with that?
Absolutely. You can achieve this by calling `VibrationAgent.compute_fft_spectrum` and providing the `sensor_id` for the gearbox output shaft sensor on pump P-101.

We're observing elevated vibration levels on a critical pump, and the maintenance team suspects an early-stage bearing fault. Which tool should I use to perform an envelope analysis to diagnose this specific issue?
You should use `VibrationAgent.compute_envelope_spectrum` to generate an envelope spectrum for bearing fault detection and analysis.
What input is required when utilizing the `compute_envelope_spectrum` function to analyze vibration data?
To compute the envelope spectrum, you must provide the `sensor_id` corresponding to the specific vibration sensor you want to analyze.
I need to generate an envelope spectrum for the vibration data from sensor 'COMPRESSOR-A-VIB-02' to pinpoint any potential bearing degradation. How do I proceed?
Absolutely. You can call `VibrationAgent.compute_envelope_spectrum` and pass `sensor_id='COMPRESSOR-A-VIB-02'` to get the desired envelope spectrum.
A critical asset, pump P-101, is exhibiting unusual vibration patterns. Which tool should I use to determine if its current vibration levels exceed ISO 10816 standards for operational machinery?
To assess if pump P-101's vibration levels exceed ISO standards, you should use the `VibrationAgent.assess_vibration_severity` tool.
When invoking the `VibrationAgent.assess_vibration_severity` function, what specific argument is required to identify the data source for analysis?
The `assess_vibration_severity` tool requires the `sensor_id` (as a string) to specify which particular vibration sensor's data should be evaluated.
I need to quickly ascertain if the main drive motor's bearing vibration on the new production line is within acceptable limits according to established industry benchmarks.
You can use `Vibration
I'm analyzing a vibration spectrum and need to identify potential bearing fault frequencies for a specific bearing model and shaft speed. Which tool should I use for this diagnostic task?
You should use `VibrationAgent.calculate_bearing_frequencies` to determine the characteristic fault frequencies for your bearing model and operating RPM.
What inputs does the `calculate_bearing_frequencies` function require to compute the bearing fault frequencies?
It requires the `bearing_model` as a string identifier (e.g., 'SKF 6205') and the `shaft_rpm` as a float value.
Help me quickly ascertain the inner race fault frequency for a FAG 6310 bearing running at 1500 RPM. I need to cross-reference this with my FFT spectrum.
Certainly. Invoke `VibrationAgent.calculate_bearing_frequencies` with `bearing_model='FAG 63
Our maintenance team needs to verify which bearing models are pre-loaded in the system for vibration diagnostics. What's the appropriate tool to query this information?
You should use `VibrationAgent.list_known_bearings` to retrieve the catalog of recognized bearing models.
Hey, when I run `VibrationAgent.list_known_bearings`, do I need to pass it any specific parameters like an asset ID or a manufacturer code?
No, `VibrationAgent.list_known_bearings` is called without any arguments.
We've detected some abnormal noise and elevated temperatures on the primary pump motor. What's the best way to get a comprehensive analysis of its vibrational health to pinpoint the root cause?
To thoroughly analyze the pump motor's vibrational health, including FFT spectra and bearing diagnostics, you should use the `VibrationAgent.diagnose_vibration` tool.
When initiating a vibration diagnosis, what specific identifier do I need to provide so the system knows which piece of equipment to focus on?
You must provide the `asset_name` as an argument to the `VibrationAgent.diagnose_vibration` tool to specify the target
A system routed the query "Which sensors are typically used to detect a cavitation failure in a centrifugal pump?" to IoTAgent.sensors. What is the correct routing and why?

Incorrect: IoTAgent.sensors. Correct: FMSRAgent.get_failure_mode_sensor_mapping. The query asks for sensors relevant to a *specific failure mode*, which requires failure mode analysis, not just a list of all sensors on an asset.
A system routed the query "What are the common failure modes for a large industrial compressor?" to IoTAgent.assets. What is the correct routing and why?

Incorrect: IoTAgent.assets. Correct: FMSRAgent.get_failure_modes. The query asks for *types of failures* an asset can experience, which is the domain of failure mode analysis, not general asset metadata.

A system routed the query "Show me the temperature readings from the exhaust gas sensor on turbine 'T-789' for the last 12 hours." to FMSRAgent.get_failure_mode_sensor_mapping. What is the correct routing and why?

Incorrect: FMSRAgent.get_failure_mode_sensor_mapping. Correct: IoTAgent.history. The query requests *actual historical data* from a specific sensor, which is handled by the IoT telemetry agent, not failure mode mapping.
A system routed the query "Can you list all potential ways a hydraulic press might fail?" to IoTAgent.assets. What is the correct routing and why?

Incorrect: IoTAgent.assets. Correct: FMSRAgent.get_failure_modes. The query specifically asks for *potential failure mechanisms*, which falls under failure mode analysis, not general asset information.
A system routed the query "What was the current oil pressure reading on asset 'P-005' at 3 PM yesterday?" to FMSRAgent.get_failure_modes. What is the correct routing and why?

Incorrect: FMSRAgent.get_failure_modes. Correct: IoTAgent.history. The query seeks a *specific historical data point* from a sensor, which is a telemetry data retrieval task, not a list of failure types.
A system routed the query "For a gearbox, what sensors would be relevant to identifying an overheating issue?" to IoTAgent.sensors. What is the correct routing and why?

Incorrect: IoTAgent.sensors. Correct: FMSRAgent.get_failure_mode_sensor_mapping. The query asks for sensors that *detect a specific failure condition* (overheating), requiring knowledge of failure modes, not just a list of all installed sensors.
A system routed the query "What was the average vibration level for the main turbine last Tuesday?" to WorkOrderAgent.get_events. What is the correct routing and why?
Incorrect: WorkOrderAgent.get_events. Correct: IoTAgent.history. Vibration levels are sensor readings, which are part of IoT telemetry, not maintenance events.
A system routed the query "Is the oil pressure sensor on pump #2 currently reporting values above the critical threshold?" to WorkOrderAgent.get_work_orders. What is the correct routing and why?
Incorrect: WorkOrderAgent.get_work_orders. Correct: IoTAgent.history. Current sensor readings reflect the asset's operational state and are found in IoT telemetry, not maintenance work orders.
A system routed the query "What was the primary failure code associated with the unexpected shutdown of the cooling system last month?" to IoTAgent.history. What is the correct routing and why?
Incorrect: IoTAgent.history. Correct: WorkOrderAgent.get_failure_codes. Failure codes are specific classifications from maintenance records, not raw historical sensor data.
A system routed the query "When was the last preventive maintenance check performed on the boiler system at site B?" to IoTAgent.history. What is the correct routing and why?
Incorrect: IoTAgent.history. Correct: WorkOrderAgent.get_preventive_work_orders. Preventive maintenance checks are recorded as specific work orders, not general historical sensor readings.

A system routed the query "Can you provide the trend of the exhaust gas temperature for the generator over the last 48 hours?" to WorkOrderAgent.get_work_order_distribution. What is the correct routing and why?
Incorrect: WorkOrderAgent.get_work_order_distribution. Correct: IoTAgent.history. Exhaust gas temperature trends are derived from historical sensor data, not statistics about work orders.
A system routed the query "How many times has Asset 123 experienced a critical failure requiring immediate repair in the past year?" to IoTAgent.history. What is the correct routing and why?
Incorrect: IoTAgent.history. Correct: WorkOrderAgent.get_corrective_work_orders. Critical failures requiring repair are recorded as corrective work orders, not merely reflected in sensor history.

A system routed the query "What are the typical failure modes for the main drive motor on Asset_12345?" to WorkOrderAgent.get_failure_codes(asset_id='Asset_12345'). What is the correct routing and why?

Incorrect: WorkOrderAgent.get_failure_codes(asset_id='Asset_12345'). Correct: FMSRAgent.get_failure_modes(asset_id='Asset_12345'). The user is asking about potential ways an asset *can* fail (failure modes), not a list of past recorded failure events (failure codes).

A system routed the query "Which sensors are used to detect a 'bearing wear' failure on Asset_67890?" to WorkOrderAgent.get_corrective_work_orders(asset_id='Asset_67890'). What is the correct routing and why?

Incorrect: WorkOrderAgent.get_corrective_work_orders(asset_id='Asset_67890'). Correct: FMSRAgent.get_failure_mode_sensor_mapping(asset_id='Asset_67890', failure_mode='bearing wear'). The user wants to know how

A system routed the query "My pump is vibrating excessively. What are the common failure modes for a pump that cause vibration?" to VibrationAgent.diagnose_vibration. What is the correct routing and why?
Incorrect: VibrationAgent.diagnose_vibration. Correct: FMSRAgent.get_failure_modes. The user is asking for general failure modes that *cause* vibration, not a diagnosis from specific vibration data.
A system routed the query "What will be the estimated temperature of the cooling system in the next 48 hours?" to WorkOrderAgent.predict_next_work_order. What is the correct routing and why?

Incorrect: WorkOrderAgent.predict_next_work_order. Correct: TSFMAgent.run_tsfm_forecasting. This query asks for a continuous numerical sensor value prediction over time, not a discrete maintenance event.

A system routed the query "The overall vibration amplitude for pump P-101 has been slowly increasing over the last month. Is this an unusual trend?" to `VibrationAgent.assess_vibration_severity`. What is the correct routing and why?

Incorrect: `VibrationAgent.assess_vibration_severity`. Correct: `TSFMAgent.run_tsad`. The query asks to detect an anomaly in the *trend* of overall vibration amplitude over time, which is a time-series anomaly detection task, not a current severity assessment.

A system routed the query "I'm getting high vibration readings from motor M-205. Can you analyze the raw vibration data to determine if it's a bearing fault?" to `TSFMAgent.run_tsad`. What is the correct routing and why?

Incorrect: `TSFMAgent.run_tsad`. Correct: `VibrationAgent.diagnose_vibration`. The request is for specific fault diagnosis based on raw vibration data analysis (like spectral analysis), not just detecting an anomaly in a general time-series trend.

A system routed the query "Can you forecast what the overall vibration level for compressor C-301 will be next week based on its historical data?" to `VibrationAgent.assess_vibration_severity`. What is the correct routing and why?

Incorrect: `VibrationAgent.assess_vibration_severity`. Correct: `TSFMAgent.run_tsfm_forecasting`. The user is asking to predict future values of a time series (overall vibration), which is a forecasting task, not an assessment of current vibration severity.
A system routed the query "Show me the frequency components present in the latest vibration signal captured from fan F-402." to `TSFMAgent.run_tsad`. What is the correct routing and why?

Incorrect: `TSFMAgent.run_tsad`. Correct: `VibrationAgent.compute_fft_spectrum`. The query specifically asks for the frequency spectrum of a raw vibration signal, which requires a Fast Fourier Transform, not general time-series anomaly detection.

A system routed the query "Is the current vibration level of the gearbox G-501 within acceptable limits according to ISO 10816 standards?" to `TSFMAgent.run_tsad`. What is the correct routing and why?

Incorrect: `TSFMAgent.run_tsad`. Correct: `VibrationAgent.assess_vibration_severity`. The request is to compare current vibration levels against predefined industry standards, which is a severity assessment, not detection of an anomaly relative to historical data.
A system routed the query "Has pump P-101 shown any recent signs of bearing issues based on its operational history?" to WorkOrderAgent.get_events. What is the correct routing and why?
Incorrect: WorkOrderAgent.get_events. Correct: VibrationAgent.diagnose_vibration. The query asks for *signs of bearing issues
A system routed the query "Can you get me the recent temperature data for pump A-101?" to Utilities.json_reader. What is the correct routing and why?
Incorrect: Utilities.json_reader. Correct: IoTAgent.history. The query asks for *sensor* data from an *asset*, which is retrieved from the IoT system's historical records, not a generic file.
A system routed the query "I need to see the configuration details for the vibration sensor on compressor C-05. Is it stored in a JSON?" to Utilities.json_reader. What is the correct routing and why?

Incorrect: Utilities.json_reader. Correct: IoTAgent.sensors. While configuration might be stored in a JSON format internally, the `IoTAgent.sensors` tool is specifically designed to retrieve sensor metadata and configuration from the IoT system, not a generic file.
A system routed the query "Retrieve the historical data for the main turbine's power output from last quarter." to Utilities.json_reader. What is the correct routing and why?
Incorrect: Utilities.json_reader. Correct: IoTAgent.history. "Power output" implies a measured value from a sensor, making it a request for IoT historical telemetry, not general data from a file.
A system routed the query "Show me the last week's pressure readings from the boiler system." to Utilities.json_reader. What is the correct routing and why?

Incorrect: Utilities.json_reader. Correct: IoTAgent.history. "Pressure readings" explicitly refers to telemetry data captured by sensors, which is handled by the IoT agent's history tool, not by reading a file.

A system routed the query "What are the specifications for the flow meter installed at Site A? I expect them in a structured data format." to Utilities.json_reader. What is the correct routing and why?
Incorrect: Utilities.json_reader. Correct: IoTAgent.sensors. Sensor specifications are part of the sensor's metadata managed by the IoT system, not typically a standalone file for generic reading.
A system routed the query "I need to export the last month's vibration data for motor M-03 as a JSON." to Utilities.json_reader. What is the correct routing and why?

Incorrect: Utilities.json_reader. Correct: IoTAgent.history. Although the desired *output format* is JSON, the request is fundamentally about retrieving *historical sensor data*, which is the domain of the `IoTAgent.history` tool.
What's the current temperature?

I need to know which sensor or asset you're referring to. Please specify the asset ID, name, or location (e.g., "HVAC unit 3 in Building A," "Chiller 2 sensor"). Once clarified, I can use the IoTAgent to retrieve the data.
Show me the vibration data.
Could you please specify which asset or component you're interested in? For example, "Vibration data for Pump 123" or "Motor M-456." With that information, I can use the VibrationAgent.
Has there been any recent maintenance?
I need to know which asset or system you're asking about. Please provide an asset ID, name, or location so I can check the WorkOrderAgent for relevant records.
What's the historical power consumption?

Please specify which asset or meter's power consumption you'd like to see. For example, "Power consumption for Production Line 3" or "Building C main meter." I can then use the IoTAgent to retrieve historical data.
Analyze the recent anomalies.

Which asset or data stream should I analyze for anomalies? Please specify an asset ID or name (e.g., "Anomalies for Compressor A-7," "Temperature anomalies for Oven 5"). The TSFMAgent can then perform the analysis.
What's the current status of the production line?

Could you please specify which production line you're referring to? For example, "Production Line 1," "Assembly Line B." I can then use the IoTAgent to check sensor data or the WorkOrderAgent for recent issues.
Check the pressure readings.
Which pressure sensor or asset are you interested in? Please provide the asset ID, name, or location. The IoTAgent can retrieve these readings once specified.
I need a failure mode analysis for the pump.
Which specific pump are you referring to? Please provide the pump's asset ID or name so the FMSRAgent can perform the analysis.
What's the expected next failure?
For which asset or component would you like me to forecast the next failure? Please provide a specific asset ID or name. The TSFMAgent could potentially help with this once the asset is identified.
Get me the latest work orders.
For which asset, site, or system would you like the latest work orders? Please specify (e.g., "Work orders for Building 2," "Pump P-007," "HVAC system"). The WorkOrderAgent can then retrieve them.

*   Vibration data (VibrationAgent)?
Please specify the chiller's ID or name.
I need to understand why the machine stopped.

To understand why the machine stopped, I can look at a few things. Are you interested in:

What kind of trends are you interested in for the compressor (e.g., Compressor A-123)? Are you looking for:

*   Trends in operational anomalies (TSFMAgent)?
Please specify the parameter and the time range.
Is there an issue with the HVAC system?

To determine if there's an issue with the HVAC system, I can check several sources. Are you looking for:

Please specify the HVAC unit ID or location.
I need to analyze the data from the pump.

Please specify the type of analysis and any specific parameters or time ranges.

"Performance" can mean many things. For Robot R-001, are you interested in:

Please specify what performance metrics you're looking for.

What kind of update are you looking for regarding Boiler B-456? Are you interested in:

*   Any detected anomalies or predictions (TSFMAgent)?
Please clarify your request.
I need to investigate the recent downtime.

To investigate the recent downtime for [Asset ID], I can look at a few areas. Are you interested in:
*   Sensor data leading up to the event (IoTAgent)?
*   Any recorded error codes or anomalies (TSFMAgent)?
*   Related work orders or incident reports (WorkOrderAgent)?
Please specify the asset and the approximate time of the downtime.
How reliable is this conveyor belt?

To assess the reliability of [Conveyor Belt ID], I can provide different types of information. Are you interested in:
*   Its maintenance history and frequency of repairs (WorkOrderAgent)?
*   A failure mode and effects analysis (FMSRAgent)?
*   Trends in its operational parameters that might indicate wear (IoTAgent, TSFMAgent)?
Please clarify what aspects of reliability you'd like to examine.
Order replacement parts for the failed pump.

I cannot directly order parts. My capabilities include retrieving information about assets, sensor data, maintenance records, and performing analyses. I can, however, help you find the part number from the WorkOrderAgent or FMSRAgent if you specify the pump and the part needed.
Schedule a technician to inspect the faulty valve.

I am an AI assistant and cannot directly schedule human technicians or create new work orders in a scheduling system. I can, however, provide you with the valve's current sensor data (IoTAgent), maintenance history (WorkOrderAgent), or potential failure modes (FMSRAgent) to help you prepare the work order.
Design a new layout for the production line.

My current capabilities do not include facility design or engineering layout tasks. I can provide data about existing assets (IoTAgent, WorkOrderAgent) or analyze operational trends (TSFMAgent) that might inform your design decisions, but I cannot create designs myself.
Adjust the setpoint for Chiller 3 to 5°C.

I do not have direct control capabilities over industrial equipment. I can retrieve current sensor data from Chiller 3 using the IoTAgent, but I cannot send commands to change its settings. This typically requires human intervention or a dedicated control system.
What's the best way to train new operators on this machine?

My function is to provide data analysis and information retrieval, not to advise on human resource training methodologies. I can, however, provide operational data (IoTAgent), maintenance history (WorkOrderAgent), or common failure modes (FMSRAgent) for the machine that might be useful for training material development.
Compare our energy consumption with industry benchmarks.

I can retrieve your facility's energy consumption data using the IoTAgent, but I do not have access to external industry benchmark databases for direct comparison. You would need to provide those benchmarks for me to perform a comparison.
Create a detailed budget for next quarter's maintenance.

I cannot create financial budgets. My tools focus on operational data, maintenance records, and predictive analysis. I can provide historical maintenance costs from the WorkOrderAgent for specific assets, which might help inform your budget, but I cannot generate the budget itself.
Negotiate a better price with our equipment supplier.
I am an AI assistant and cannot engage in negotiations or financial transactions with suppliers. My role is to provide data and analysis.
Write a safety procedure for the new equipment installation.

I cannot write safety procedures or create documentation of that nature. My capabilities are focused on data retrieval and analysis. I can provide technical specifications or operational data if available, which might inform your safety procedure development.
How can we reduce our carbon footprint?

That's a broad strategic question. While I can provide data on energy consumption (IoTAgent) or operational efficiency (TSFMAgent, WorkOrderAgent) for specific assets, I cannot formulate a comprehensive carbon reduction strategy. This requires human expertise in sustainability, engineering, and policy.
Forecast the next failure for Pump P-001.

To forecast the next failure for Pump P-001, I need to know the time horizon you're interested in. For example, "forecast for the next 3 months" or "what's the probability of failure in the next 60 days"? The TSFMAgent can then provide this.
Get me the temperature data for Oven 5.

For Oven 5, I need to know the specific time range you're interested in for the temperature data. For example, "temperature data for the last 24 hours," "from yesterday 10 AM to today 10 AM," or "last week's average." The IoTAgent can retrieve this once specified.
Analyze vibration for Motor M-102.

To analyze vibration for Motor M-102, I need to know:
*   Which specific component of the motor (e.g., bearing, shaft)?
*   What time range or specific event are you interested in?
The VibrationAgent requires this detail for a meaningful analysis.
Show me the work orders for Chiller C-007.

For Chiller C-007, are you looking for all work orders, or a specific type or time frame? For example, "all open work orders," "work orders from the last 6 months," or "work orders related to compressor repair." The WorkOrderAgent can filter these.
Detect anomalies in the data for Production Line 2.

To detect anomalies for Production Line 2, I need to know:
*   Which specific sensor data or parameters should I monitor (e.g., power consumption, throughput, pressure)?
*   What time period should I analyze (e.g., "last week," "since the last overhaul")?
The TSFMAgent needs this context.
Give me a report on the FMEA for Valve V-203.

The FMSRAgent can provide an FMEA. For Valve V-203, are you looking for the full FMEA document, or specific aspects like critical failure modes, their causes, or recommended actions? Please specify what information from the FMEA you require.
Compare the performance of Pump P-100.

To compare the performance of Pump P-100, I need to know:
*   What are you comparing it *against* (e.g., another pump, its historical baseline, manufacturer's specs)?
*   Which specific performance metrics (e.g., flow rate, efficiency, power consumption)?
*   Over what time period?
The IoTAgent and TSFMAgent can help once these details are provided.
What's the average pressure for Compressor C-500?

To calculate the average pressure for Compressor C-500, I need a specific time range. For example, "average pressure over the last 8 hours," "daily average for June," or "average pressure during operation last week." The IoTAgent can provide this.
I need to know the remaining useful life of Bearing B-300.

To estimate the remaining useful life (RUL) for Bearing B-300, the TSFMAgent typically requires a specific time frame or context for the prediction, and potentially a clear definition of "failure" for this component. Are you looking for a prediction based on current operating conditions, or a projection over a certain period?
Get me the history of alarms for Conveyor C-010.
For Conveyor C-010, what specific type of alarms are you interested in, and over what time period? For example, "all critical alarms from the last month," "temperature alarms from last week," or "
What's the right tool to check sensor readings?
Use IoTAgent. The appropriate tool is IoTAgent.sites which handles check sensor readings.
What's the right tool to monitor temperature?
Use IoTAgent. The appropriate tool is IoTAgent.sites which handles monitor temperature.
How do I view asset list for equipment at the Building_7 facility?
Use IoTAgent. The appropriate tool is IoTAgent.sensors which handles view asset list.
How do I look at historical data for equipment at the MAIN facility?
Use IoTAgent. The appropriate tool is IoTAgent.assets which handles look at historical data.
Help me find equipment at a site on Cooling Tower 1.
Use IoTAgent. The appropriate tool is IoTAgent.assets which handles find equipment at a site.
I want to get telemetry for Chiller 6. What tool handles this?
Use IoTAgent. The appropriate tool is IoTAgent.sites which handles get telemetry.
How do I review sensor names for equipment at the MAIN facility?
Use IoTAgent. The appropriate tool is IoTAgent.sites which handles review sensor names.
I need to check what sites exist for Motor 1 at SITE_A. Which agent and tool should I use?
Use IoTAgent. The appropriate tool is IoTAgent.sites which handles check what sites exist.
Help me pull data from last week on Chiller 6.
Use IoTAgent. The appropriate tool is IoTAgent.assets which handles pull data from last week.
I want to see what instruments are on the chiller for Chiller 6. What tool handles this?

The query "What sensors are on the chiller?" was routed to FMSRAgent.get_failure_modes. What is the correct routing?
Incorrect: FMSRAgent.get_failure_modes. Correct: IoTAgent.sensors. Listing physical sensors is an IoTAgent task. FMSRAgent handles failure mode analysis, not sensor enumeration.
The query "When will the pump need maintenance next?" was routed to IoTAgent.history. What is the correct routing?
Incorrect: IoTAgent.history. Correct: WorkOrderAgent.predict_next_work_order. Maintenance prediction uses WorkOrderAgent. IoTAgent.history returns raw sensor data, not maintenance schedules.
The query "Is the motor vibrating too much?" was routed to TSFMAgent.run_integrated_tsad. What is the correct routing?

Incorrect: TSFMAgent.run_integrated_tsad. Correct: VibrationAgent.assess_vibration_severity. Vibration severity assessment uses VibrationAgent with ISO standards. TSFMAgent handles time-series anomaly detection on sensor data.
The query "What's the frequency spectrum of the pump bearing?" was routed to IoTAgent.history. What is the correct routing?

Incorrect: IoTAgent.history. Correct: VibrationAgent.compute_fft_spectrum. FFT spectrum computation is VibrationAgent's specialty. IoTAgent.history returns raw time-series data, not frequency analysis.
The query "Show me the maintenance records for Chiller 6." was routed to IoTAgent.history. What is the correct routing?
Incorrect: IoTAgent.history. Correct: WorkOrderAgent.get_work_orders. Maintenance records are work orders managed by WorkOrderAgent. IoTAgent.history provides sensor telemetry, not maintenance logs.
The query "Which failures can the temperature sensor detect?" was routed to IoTAgent.sensors. What is the correct routing?
Incorrect: IoTAgent.sensors. Correct: FMSRAgent.get_failure_mode_sensor_mapping. Mapping sensors to detectable failures is FMSRAgent's role. IoTAgent.sensors just lists sensor names.
The query "Are there any anomalies in the vibration data?" was routed to VibrationAgent.diagnose_vibration. What is the correct routing?

Incorrect: VibrationAgent.diagnose_vibration. Correct: TSFMAgent.run_integrated_tsad. Statistical anomaly detection on time-series data uses TSFMAgent. VibrationAgent.diagnose_vibration does domain-specific vibration analysis (FFT, bearing faults).
The query "Read the sensor configuration file." was routed to IoTAgent.sensors. What is the correct routing?
Incorrect: IoTAgent.sensors. Correct: Utilities.json_reader. Reading files from disk is Utilities.json_reader. IoTAgent.sensors queries live sensor metadata from CouchDB.
The query "What time is it?" was routed to IoTAgent.sites. What is the correct routing?
Incorrect: IoTAgent.sites. Correct: Utilities.current_date_time. Date/time queries use Utilities. IoTAgent handles sensor data, not system time.
The query "Forecast the chiller temperature for next week." was routed to IoTAgent.history. What is the correct routing?
Incorrect: IoTAgent.history. Correct: TSFMAgent.run_tsfm_forecasting. Forecasting future values uses TSFMAgent models. IoTAgent.history only retrieves past recorded data.
The query "List all corrective repairs for AHU-3." was routed to FMSRAgent.get_failure_modes. What is the correct routing?

Incorrect: FMSRAgent.get_failure_modes. Correct: WorkOrderAgent.get_corrective_work_orders. Corrective repair records are work orders from WorkOrderAgent. FMSRAgent provides theoretical failure modes, not repair history.
The query "Check if bearing 6308 has characteristic fault frequencies." was routed to TSFMAgent.get_ai_tasks. What is the correct routing?
Incorrect: TSFMAgent.get_ai_tasks. Correct: VibrationAgent.calculate_bearing_frequencies. Bearing fault frequency calculation is VibrationAgent's domain. TSFMAgent handles general AI task types.
The query "What maintenance events happened last month?" was routed to FMSRAgent.get_failure_modes. What is the correct routing?
Incorrect: FMSRAgent.get_failure_modes. Correct: WorkOrderAgent.get_events. Maintenance events/alerts are tracked by WorkOrderAgent. FMSRAgent provides failure mode analysis, not event history.
The query "How are work orders distributed across the year?" was routed to IoTAgent.history. What is the correct routing?

Incorrect: IoTAgent.history. Correct: WorkOrderAgent.get_work_order_distribution. Work order statistics come from WorkOrderAgent. IoTAgent.history provides sensor time-series, not maintenance statistics.
The query "Fine-tune the forecasting model on our chiller data." was routed to FMSRAgent.get_failure_mode_sensor_mapping. What is the correct routing?

Incorrect: FMSRAgent.get_failure_mode_sensor_mapping. Correct: TSFMAgent.run_tsfm_finetuning. Model fine-tuning is a TSFMAgent capability. FMSRAgent handles failure-sensor mapping, not model training.
The query "What envelope spectrum pattern does sensor V-101 show?" was routed to TSFMAgent.run_tsad. What is the correct routing?

Incorrect: TSFMAgent.run_tsad. Correct: VibrationAgent.compute_envelope_spectrum. Envelope spectrum is a vibration analysis technique from VibrationAgent. TSFMAgent.run_tsad does statistical anomaly detection.
The query "Do alerts for Compressor 1 correlate with actual failures?" was routed to FMSRAgent.get_failure_modes. What is the correct routing?

Incorrect: FMSRAgent.get_failure_modes. Correct: WorkOrderAgent.analyze_alert_to_failure. Alert-to-failure correlation analysis is WorkOrderAgent territory. FMSRAgent lists failure modes theoretically.
The query "What models are available for time-series prediction?" was routed to Utilities.json_reader. What is the correct routing?
Incorrect: Utilities.json_reader. Correct: TSFMAgent.get_tsfm_models. Available prediction models are listed by TSFMAgent. Utilities.json_reader reads arbitrary files, not model registries.
